title: The Extra Day That Only Shows Up Every Four Years
story: February 29 is the rarest day on the calendar because it only appears once every four years! This special day is called Leap Day, and it exists to keep our calendars in sync with Earth's orbit around the sun. People born on February 29 are called "leaplings" and they throw epic birthday parties since they can only celebrate their real birthday every four years. Some leaplings joke that they age four times slower than everyone else! Many people use Leap Day as a chance to do something they've never done before, like trying a new hobby or visiting a new place. It's like getting a bonus day to make extra memories.
activity_question: What are people born on February 29 called?
follow_up_question: What special thing would you do if you had an extra day every four years?

title: Rare Disease Day: Standing Together Strong
story: Every February 29, people around the world wear bright colors and share stories to support families affected by rare diseases. This special day happens on the rarest date of the calendar because rare diseases need extra attention too! Schools, hospitals, and communities organize walks, fundraisers, and awareness events. People post photos with zebra stripes online since doctors say "when you hear hoofbeats, think horses, not zebras" - but sometimes it really is a zebra!
activity_question: Why is the zebra used as a symbol for rare diseases?
activity_choices: Because zebras are the rarest animals in the world
Because doctors say rare diseases are like zebras - unexpected but real
Because zebra stripes look like DNA patterns
Because zebras only come out on leap years
activity_answer: Because doctors say rare diseases are like zebras - unexpected but real
bonus_fact: There are over 7,000 different rare diseases affecting 300 million people worldwide.

title: Jazz Legend Jimmy Dorsey's Leap Year Birth
story: Swing into the world of jazz! On February 29, 1904, Jimmy Dorsey made his grand entrance in Shenandoah, Pennsylvania. This leap year baby grew up to become a musical powerhouse. Dorsey mastered the saxophone and clarinet, composed catchy tunes, and led a famous big band. His hits like 'Green Eyes' and 'Tangerine' had everyone tapping their toes.
activity_question: What instruments did Jimmy Dorsey play?
bonus_fact: Jimmy Dorsey's birthday only comes once every four years because he was born on leap day!

title: Al Rosen: Baseball's Mighty Slugger
story: Bam! The crack of the bat echoed through the stadium. Al Rosen, born in 1924 on February 29, became a baseball powerhouse. This Cleveland Indians star shone bright, earning All-Star status for years. Rosen's talent led him to World Series glory and even snagged him the American League's Most Valuable Player award. After hanging up his glove, he continued to make waves in baseball management.
activity_question: What team did Al Rosen play for?
bonus_fact: Al Rosen was born on a leap day, meaning his birthday only comes once every four years!

title: Math Whiz Born on Leap Day
story: Gene Golub, a brilliant mathematician, entered the world on February 29, 1932. Born in Chicago, Illinois, Golub would grow up to become famous for his work in numerical analysis. This branch of math helps solve complex problems using computers. Golub's leap year birthday made him extra special in the world of numbers!
activity_question: What field of mathematics was Gene Golub known for?
follow_up_question: How often does a leap year occur?

title: A Sweet Voice Joins the World
story: A special voice entered the world on February 29, 1940. Gretchen Christopher was born in Olympia, Washington. She would grow up to become a talented singer and a member of the popular music group The Fleetwoods. Her unique vocals helped create the group's signature sound, delighting fans with their harmonies.
activity_question: In which city was Gretchen Christopher born?
follow_up_question: What was the name of the music group Gretchen Christopher joined?

title: Rhythms and Melodies: A Composer's Birth
story: Drumbeats echoed through Wellington as Gareth Farr entered the world. Born on February 29, 1968, this New Zealand native would grow up to become a talented percussionist and composer. Farr's musical journey led him to create powerful pieces like 'From the Depths Sound the Great Sea Gongs' and 'Chemin des Dames', blending his percussion skills with innovative compositions.
activity_question: What type of musician is Gareth Farr?
follow_up_question: Can you name one of Gareth Farr's musical compositions mentioned in the story?

title: Soccer Star Taylor Twellman Kicks Off Life
story: In Minneapolis, Minnesota, a future soccer sensation took his first breath. Taylor Twellman was born in 1980, destined to become a star forward. He would go on to play for the New England Revolution and earn 30 caps representing the United States national team. Twellman's journey from a February 29 birthday to soccer stardom shows how leap year babies can make big leaps in sports!
activity_question: Which professional soccer team did Taylor Twellman play for?
follow_up_question: What does 'caps' mean in soccer?
